name: adyen
description: Create Adyen payment sessions, check session results, list payment methods, and issue refunds via the Checkout API

envVars.name: ADYEN_API_KEY
envVars.description: Adyen API key generated for your API credential in the Customer Area (Developers > API credentials)

envVars.name: ADYEN_MERCHANT_ACCOUNT
envVars.description: Adyen merchant account name, passed as the merchantAccount field in API requests

tools.name: Create Payment Session
tools.description: Create an Adyen Checkout payment session that a Drop-in or Component can use to collect payment

checkoutHost.description: Adyen Checkout API host. Use checkout-test.adyen.com for test; for live use your prefixed host including the checkout path segment, e.g. {prefix}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout

amount.description: Amount object with currency (ISO 4217 code) and value in minor units, e.g. {"currency": "EUR", "value": 1000} for EUR 10.00

merchantAccount.description: Adyen merchant account name to process the payment with

reference.description: Your unique reference for this payment

returnUrl.description: URL the shopper returns to after completing the payment

countryCode.description: Shopper's two-letter country code, used to determine available payment methods

shopperReference.description: Your unique shopper identifier, required for recurring payments

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 session expiry; defaults to 1 hour after creation, maximum 24 hours

tools.name: Get Session Result
tools.description: Get the final result of an Adyen payment session, including authorized payments and status

checkoutHost.description: Adyen Checkout API host. Use checkout-test.adyen.com for test; for live use your prefixed host including the checkout path segment, e.g. {prefix}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout

sessionId.description: Unique identifier of the payment session

sessionResult.description: The sessionResult value returned by the Drop-in or Component

tools.name: List Payment Methods
tools.description: List the payment methods available for a merchant account, amount, and shopper country

checkoutHost.description: Adyen Checkout API host. Use checkout-test.adyen.com for test; for live use your prefixed host including the checkout path segment, e.g. {prefix}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout

amount.description: Optional amount object with currency and value in minor units to filter methods

channel.description: Sales channel: Web, iOS, or Android

shopperReference.description: Your unique shopper identifier, used to return stored payment methods

tools.name: Create Payment Link
tools.description: Create a hosted Pay by Link URL the shopper can open to pay, without needing a Drop-in or Component frontend

checkoutHost.description: Adyen Checkout API host. Use checkout-test.adyen.com for test; for live use your prefixed host including the checkout path segment, e.g. {prefix}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout

amount.description: Amount object with currency (ISO 4217 code) and value in minor units, e.g. {"currency": "EUR", "value": 1000} for EUR 10.00

merchantAccount.description: Adyen merchant account name to process the payment with

reference.description: Your unique reference for this payment link

description.description: Description shown to the shopper on the payment page

countryCode.description: Shopper's two-letter country code, used to determine available payment methods

shopperReference.description: Your unique shopper identifier, required for recurring payments

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 expiry of the link; defaults to 24 hours after creation

reusable.description: Whether the link can be used for multiple payments (defaults to false)

returnUrl.description: URL the shopper is redirected to after completing the payment

tools.name: Capture Payment
tools.description: Capture an authorised Adyen payment (for manual-capture flows); the outcome arrives asynchronously via a CAPTURE webhook

checkoutHost.description: Adyen Checkout API host. Use checkout-test.adyen.com for test; for live use your prefixed host including the checkout path segment, e.g. {prefix}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout

paymentPspReference.description: PSP reference of the authorised payment to capture

merchantAccount.description: Adyen merchant account name the payment was processed with

amount.description: Amount to capture with currency and value in minor units; less than the authorised amount for a partial capture

reference.description: Your unique reference for this capture request

tools.name: Create Refund
tools.description: Refund all or part of a captured Adyen payment; the outcome arrives asynchronously via a REFUND webhook

checkoutHost.description: Adyen Checkout API host. Use checkout-test.adyen.com for test; for live use your prefixed host including the checkout path segment, e.g. {prefix}-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout

paymentPspReference.description: PSP reference of the payment to refund

merchantAccount.description: Adyen merchant account name the payment was processed with

amount.description: Amount to refund with currency and value in minor units, e.g. {"currency": "EUR", "value": 500}

reference.description: Your unique reference for this refund request

merchantRefundReason.description: Reason for the refund, such as FRAUD, CUSTOMER REQUEST, RETURN, DUPLICATE, or OTHER

prompts.title: Start a payment session
prompts.prompt: Create an Adyen payment session for a given amount and currency, and give me the session details.

prompts.title: Check session result
prompts.prompt: Check the result of an Adyen payment session and summarize whether the payment succeeded.

prompts.title: Issue a refund
prompts.prompt: Refund an Adyen payment by PSP reference: confirm the amount with me first, then submit the refund.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Adyen setup

steps.title: Create an Adyen test account
steps.description: Sign up for a free Adyen test account at https://www.adyen.com/signup. This gives you a test Customer Area at https://ca-test.adyen.com with a test merchant account.

steps.title: Generate an API key
steps.description: In the Customer Area, go to Developers > API credentials, select (or create) a credential, then under Server settings > Authentication open the API key tab and select Generate API key. Copy it immediately - it cannot be shown again after you leave the page.

steps.title: Configure environment variables
steps.description: Set ADYEN_API_KEY to the generated key. Optionally set ADYEN_MERCHANT_ACCOUNT to your merchant account name (shown in the Customer Area) - it is required as the merchantAccount field in every request body.

steps.title: Going live
steps.description: Live mode needs a separate API key generated in the live Customer Area (https://ca-live.adyen.com), and live API calls use your unique live URL prefix. Find the prefix under Developers > API URLs in the live Customer Area.

notes: Each tool takes a checkoutHost parameter. For test, keep the default checkout-test.adyen.com. For live, pass your prefixed live host including the checkout path segment, e.g. 1797a841fbb37ca7-AdyenDemo-checkout-live.adyenpayments.com/checkout (test and live URLs have different structures).
Amounts are in minor units (e.g. value 1000 with currency EUR is EUR 10.00).
Refund outcomes are asynchronous: the API acknowledges the request and the final result is delivered via a REFUND webhook.
The Checkout API does not provide a payment listing endpoint; use get_session_result or webhooks to track payment outcomes.
